title: Chapter 5: The Hunt Begins
content: Three days after the poisoning, Kael called a pack hunt.

This was not unusual — the pack hunted every full moon, and the moon was waxing toward fullness — but the *timing* was. Alphas who had nearly died from wolfsbane did not typically lead hunts three days later. They rested. They recovered. They let their Betas handle things.
Kael, apparently, had not received that particular memo.
"He's insane," Sera muttered, watching from the castle window as the hunting party assembled in the courtyard below. "Certifiably insane."
*"I can HEAR you judging me from up there. Well, I can't HEAR you, but I can FEEL you judging me. Judging energy. It's a thing."*
Sera bit her lip. She was getting too comfortable with the voices in her head. That was probably a bad sign.

The hunting party was impressive. Thirty wolves, all in human form for now, armed with bows and blades. Darius stood at Kael's right, his grey eyes scanning the tree line with professional paranoia. Lady Isolde was there too — freshly released from her stable duty, her auburn hair gleaming in the morning sun, her smile fixed firmly in place despite the lingering smell of horse that no amount of perfume could quite cover.
And Kael.

He looked... recovered. More than recovered. His black hair was tied back in its usual severe style, his military coat immaculate, his golden eyes sharp and clear. If Sera hadn't watched him almost die three days ago, she would never have guessed anything was wrong.

*"My legs still feel like they're made of wet paper. But does anyone need to know that? NO. Because I am the ALPHA. Alphas do not have wet-paper legs. Alphas have STEEL legs. LEGS OF STEEL. That are currently screaming in agony but in a very STEEL way."*
Sera pressed her forehead against the cold window glass.
"Sera."
She turned. Elder Magnus stood behind her, his pale eyes twinkling.
"You should be downstairs," he said. "The Alpha requested your presence at the hunt."
"My presence? I'm a maid."
"You are the Alpha's personal maid. And the Alpha's personal maid attends the Alpha's personal hunts." Magnus's beard twitched with what might have been amusement. "Pack tradition. Very important."
"That's not a real tradition."
"It is now." The old wolf turned to go, then paused. "The forest holds answers, child. For those who know how to listen."
And then he was gone, because apparently no conversation with Elder Magnus was complete without at least one cryptic statement.
The Blackwood Forest was different from the castle.

The castle was stone and shadow and politics. The forest was *alive*. Ancient oaks stretched toward the sky like grasping fingers. Moss cushioned every footstep. The air smelled of pine and damp earth and something wilder underneath — something that made the creature in Sera's chest twitch in its sleep.

She walked at the rear of the hunting party, as befitted a servant. The warriors ranged ahead, fanning out through the trees in a loose formation. Kael led from the front, his long strides eating up the forest floor, his golden eyes scanning the shadows for prey.

*"I can feel her behind me. Watching me. Judging my walking. My walking is FINE. It's a NORMAL Alpha walk. Strong. Confident. Not at ALL like someone who was paralyzed three days ago and is currently running on pure spite and three cups of extremely strong tea."*
Sera hid her smile behind her hand.

The hunt was technically for deer — a massive buck that had been spotted near the eastern ridge — but Sera suspected the real prey was information. Kael was scanning the forest for signs of Aldric's remaining supporters. Every broken branch, every disturbed patch of moss earned a sharp glance and a silent hand signal to Darius.
They found the first body an hour into the hunt.
It was one of Aldric's wolves — a young male, barely out of adolescence, his throat torn out by something much larger than a wolf. Kael knelt beside the corpse, his expression unreadable.
"Three days dead," he murmured. "The wound pattern is wrong. Wolves don't kill like this."
*"Something else is in the forest. Something that wasn't here before. Something BIG. Whatever killed this kid, it wasn't one of ours. And it wasn't Aldric's people — they wouldn't kill their own."*
Darius crouched beside him. "Rogues?"
"No." Kael's voice was flat. "Rogues leave messes. This was clean. Efficient. Whatever did this..." He touched the edges of the wound. "...knew exactly where to strike."
A cold finger traced down Sera's spine.
The creature in her chest stirred again. Harder this time. Almost... alert.
*Danger*, it seemed to say. *Close.*
"Alpha." Isolde's voice cut through the tension. "Perhaps we should return to the castle. If there's an unknown predator in the forest — "
"The pack does not flee from unknown predators." Kael rose, his golden eyes sweeping the tree line. "We hunt them."

*"That sounded really cool. Did that sound cool? I hope it sounded cool. Sera, did that sound cool? Why am I asking her, she can't hear me. Probably. Almost certainly. Unless she CAN, which would explain a LOT of things, but I've decided not to think about that because it makes my brain hurt and right now my brain needs to focus on the MYSTERY KILLER in the FOREST."*
Sera's heart hammered. He was getting closer to the truth. Every day, every interaction, he edged nearer to figuring out that she could hear him.
She needed to tell him. Soon. Before he figured it out on his own and felt betrayed.
But not today. Today there was a dead wolf in the forest and something dangerous watching from the shadows.
They found the second body at noon.
This one was fresher — maybe a day old — and it was one of the pack's own scouts. A grizzled veteran named Thorn who had been patrolling the eastern border.
Same wound pattern. Same clean kill. Same expression of absolute shock frozen on his dead face.
"He never saw it coming," Darius said quietly. "Thorn was one of our best. Whatever killed him..."
"Was faster," Kael finished. "And it's still here."
He turned to face the hunting party. "We're being hunted. Form defensive positions. No one goes anywhere alone. Darius, take the eastern flank. Isolde — "
A blur of motion in the trees.
Sera saw it before anyone else — a flash of grey fur, too large to be a normal wolf, moving through the shadows with unnatural speed. It was heading straight for the cluster of warriors.
"There!" she shouted, pointing. "In the trees!"
The warriors spun, weapons raised. But the thing was already among them.

It was a wolf — but wrong. Too large, for one thing. Its shoulders came up to a man's chest. Its fur was the color of old ash, patchy and mottled, and its eyes — its eyes were *empty*. Not blind. Empty. Like whatever had once lived behind them was long gone, replaced by something that had never been alive.
A *rogue*. But not the kind Kael had dismissed.
This was something much, much worse.

The rogue wolf hit the first warrior before anyone could react. One swipe of its massive paw sent the man flying into a tree trunk with a sickening crack. The second warrior managed to get his blade up — and then the rogue's jaws closed around his arm and *twisted*, and the blade went spinning into the underbrush along with several things that should not have been separated from a body.
"FORM UP!" Kael's roar cut through the chaos. His human form was already rippling, fur pushing through skin, bones cracking and reshaping. "PROTECT THE WOUNDED!"

The black wolf that erupted from Kael's body was magnificent. Twice the size of a natural wolf, with fur like liquid shadow and eyes like burning gold. He launched himself at the rogue with a snarl that shook leaves from the trees.
*"I HAVE NO IDEA WHAT THIS THING IS BUT IT HURT MY PACK AND NOW IT DIES. DARIUS! LEFT FLANK! ISOLDE! STOP STANDING THERE AND HELP! SERA! STAY BACK! STAY BACK STAY BACK STAY BACK — "*
Sera pressed herself against a tree trunk, her heart trying to claw its way out of her chest. She was useless here. Wolf-less. Defenseless. A liability.
The battle was chaos.

Kael and the rogue wolf clashed in the center of the clearing — a whirlwind of black fur and grey, of snapping jaws and slashing claws. Every impact shook the ground. Every snarl vibrated in Sera's bones. Darius circled the fight, looking for an opening. The remaining warriors had formed a protective ring around the wounded.
And Isolde —
Isolde was backing away. Slowly, deliberately, her eyes fixed not on the rogue but on Sera.
*She's going to let me die*, Sera realized with cold clarity. *If the rogue breaks through, she'll let it kill me and claim she couldn't reach me in time.*
The rogue broke through.
Kael's jaws had closed on its throat, but the creature twisted with impossible flexibility, ripping free with a spray of black blood, and lunged —
Straight at Sera.
Time slowed.
She saw the rogue's empty eyes. Saw the drool stringing from its jaws. Saw the claws, each one the length of her finger, reaching for her chest.
And then she saw Kael.
The black wolf was already moving, already launching himself between her and the rogue, but he was too far, too slow, the distance was too great —
*"NO! NO NO NO — I WON'T LET IT — SHE CAN'T — SERA!"*
The thing in Sera's chest woke up.
Not slowly. Not gently. It *exploded*.

Heat flooded her body — not the gentle warmth of before but a *conflagration*, a wildfire, a supernova compressed into human form. Every nerve screamed. Every muscle seized. Her vision went silver-white, and she felt something inside her *stretch* — something that had been curled tight for twenty-two years suddenly reaching, uncoiling, *demanding* to be released.
Her birthmark burned like a brand.
And then the rogue wolf hit her —
— and bounced.
The creature flew backward as if it had struck a stone wall. It crashed into a tree, dazed, shaking its massive head. In the space where Sera had been standing, there was now...
Light.
Silver light, pouring from her skin like liquid moonlight.
"What the — " Darius's voice was stunned. "She's — "
"*Transforming.*" Magnus's voice came from somewhere behind them — the old wolf had followed the hunting party, because of course he had. "Don't interfere. Don't touch her. Let it happen."

Sera couldn't hear them. She couldn't hear anything over the roaring in her ears — the roaring of her own blood, her own power, her own *wolf* screaming its first scream into the world after two decades of silence.
Her bones cracked. Her skin rippled. Her clothes shredded — she'd care about that later — and where a silver-haired maid had been standing, there was now a wolf.
A silver wolf.

Smaller than Kael. Smaller than the rogue. But *wrong* in a completely different way — because she wasn't a natural wolf. She was a wolf made of moonlight. Her fur glowed faintly, silver-white and luminous, and her eyes — when she opened them — were the color of mercury.
The rogue wolf stared at her.
Sera — the wolf Sera, the silver Sera, the *awakened* Sera — stared back.
And then she moved.

She wasn't fast in the way Kael was fast — all brute power and Alpha fury. She was fast in the way *light* was fast. One heartbeat she was standing by the tree; the next, she was at the rogue's throat.
Her jaws closed. Silver teeth met grey fur. And the rogue wolf — the creature that had shrugged off Kael's Alpha strength — *screamed*.
It was a sound no living thing should make. A sound of wrongness being *unmade*.

The rogue crumbled. Not died — *crumbled*. Its body dissolved into ash and shadow and something that looked like smoke trying to escape sunlight. Within seconds, there was nothing left but a dark smear on the forest floor.
Sera stood over the remains, silver fur still glowing, mercury eyes still blazing.
And then the light went out.
Her body collapsed — shrinking, reshaping, folding back into human form — and she hit the mossy ground naked and trembling and absolutely certain she was about to die of sheer shock.
Something warm wrapped around her. A coat. *The* coat. The black military coat with silver buttons.

Kael — human again, his own transformation reversed — knelt beside her, his coat draped over her shoulders, his golden eyes burning with something that was definitely not cold or controlled or remotely Alpha-like.
"You," he said, his voice rough with something that might have been awe, "are full of surprises."

*"SHE'S A WOLF. A SILVER WOLF. A MOONLIGHT WOLF. THE MOST BEAUTIFUL WOLF I'VE EVER SEEN. I'M GOING TO — I don't even know what I'm going to do. Cry? Howl? Write a THOUSAND poems? SHE TRANSFORMED TO SAVE ME. Okay, technically she transformed to save HERSELF, but she transformed because she was in danger and she was in danger because she was HELPING US and that COUNTS. THAT ABSOLUTELY COUNTS."*
Sera tried to speak. All that came out was a tiny, breathless laugh.
"Surprises," she managed. "That's... one word for it."
Kael's lips twitched — *twitched* — and for the first time since she'd met him, the Wolf King almost smiled.
"Rest," he said. "We'll talk when you can stand."
He lifted her — coat and all — and carried her out of the forest like she weighed nothing at all.

*"I'm carrying her. I'm actually carrying her. Her head is on my shoulder. Her hair is touching my neck. She smells like moonlight. Not lavender — MOONLIGHT. How does someone smell like moonlight?! That's not even a SMELL. But she DOES. She smells like silver and magic and — and she just TURNED INTO A WOLF to protect US. To protect ME. No one's ever done that before. No one's ever — I'm going to marry her. I'm going to marry her SO HARD. After we deal with the murder-rogue situation. And the traitor-wolves. And the pack politics. But THEN. THEN I'm going to marry her."*
Sera closed her eyes, wrapped in the Wolf King's coat and the Wolf King's arms and the Wolf King's utterly unhinged inner monologue, and let herself smile.
The Moon-Marked maiden and the Alpha with two hearts.
What could possibly go wrong?
